name: Reduce DotAgents system prompt tokens while preserving constraint-retry effectiveness

description: Baseline prompt-token benchmark initialized from merged constraint-preserving prompt.

asi.hypothesis: Establish the new objective and baseline: measure the actual assembled system prompt tokens while preserving the existing constraint-preserving retry replay behavior.
asi.learned: Baseline assembled system prompt is about 2472 regex tokens / 11900 chars. Live effectiveness was 0.464 on this noisy single run, below the 0.65 floor, so the primary metric includes a 9300 effectiveness penalty. Future candidates need reduce tokens without worsening this e2e score and ideally recover above the floor.

description: Compact base prompt local memory and DotAgents config sections into one shorter section.

asi.hypothesis: The duplicated base prompt sections for knowledge notes, past conversations, and DotAgents config can be compressed into a single local-memory/config section because agent-mode already injects similar guidance later.
asi.learned: Tokens dropped from 2472 to 2200 (-272, about 11%), but live effectiveness fell from 0.464 to 0.402, increasing the floor penalty enough to worsen the primary metric. This broad base-section compression may remove useful retrieval/config detail or simply be within live-model noise, but it is not safe to keep as-is.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened from 11772 to 14600 because the effectiveness penalty increased despite lower prompt tokens.
asi.next_action_hint: Try smaller, lower-risk compression in duplicated agent-mode sections or sections less tied to replay behavior, and preserve concrete past-conversation/config retrieval details until a candidate beats the baseline.

asi.hypothesis: The respond_to_user section contains channel/image details that are not needed on every agent-mode turn; a shorter instruction can preserve direct user messaging behavior while reducing system prompt tokens.
asi.learned: Shortening RESPONDING TO USER reduced the assembled prompt by 55 tokens / 306 chars and the live e2e score improved to 0.694, clearing the effectiveness floor. This is a low-risk compression candidate, though the large e2e improvement may include live-model variance; consider a confirmation rerun or continue with similarly small prompt-section compactions.
asi.next_action_hint: Next try compacting another isolated low-risk section such as COMPLETION SIGNAL, or rerun the kept candidate unchanged if confidence/noise remains uncertain.

description: Compact the respond_to_user plus mark_work_complete completion-signal guidance.

asi.hypothesis: The common completion-signal branch can be shorter while preserving the essential ordering: final answer via respond_to_user first, then mark_work_complete only when fully complete, with no duplicate recap.
asi.learned: Prompt size improved from 2417 to 2392 tokens (-25) and effectiveness stayed above the 0.65 floor at 0.656 with zero penalty. This is a small but clean compression on top of the prior respond_to_user simplification.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with small isolated prompt-section compactions. Candidate next sections: skills guidance (preserve registry-refresh behavior), status/continuation wording (higher risk), or execute_command file-reading duplication (larger token upside but more behavioral risk).

description: Compact skills guidance while preserving registry-refresh behavior.

asi.hypothesis: The skills section can be shortened without losing the important behavior: load each skill at most once, reuse successful loads, diagnose on-disk-but-unloadable skills as runtime registry/refresh problems before recreating files, and avoid guessing skill contents.
asi.learned: Prompt size improved from 2392 to 2359 tokens (-33) and live effectiveness remained above the floor at 0.678 with lower extra tool calls. The concise skills wording preserved the registry-refresh behavior well enough for the replay while reducing prompt size.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue small isolated compactions. Best candidates: trim duplicated execute_command/file-reading guidance for larger token savings, or compress STATUS & CONTINUATION carefully because it is behavior-critical but verbose.
